article_title: Bylaw 14777 — Burnaby Piggery Location Bylaw 1921, Repeal Bylaw 2025

minutes_briefs.title: Burnaby repeals 1921 bylaw that restricted pig farm locations
minutes_briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has repealed Bylaw No. 317, the Burnaby Piggery Location By-Law from 1921. This old bylaw controlled where pig farms (piggeries) could be located in the city. Repealing it means those specific location rules no longer exist. This change removes an outdated law that likely had no practical effect on modern businesses, since pig farming is no longer common in Burnaby.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Bylaw No. 14777 
Page 1 
CITY OF BURNABY 
BYLAW NO. 14777 
A BYLAW to repeal 
Burnaby Piggery Location 
By-Law, 1921 
The Council of the City of Burnaby ENACTS as follows: 
1.
This Bylaw may be cited as BURNABY PIGGERY LOCATION BY-LAW, 1921,
REPEAL BYLAW 2025.
2.
Bylaw No. 317, being Burnaby Piggery Location By-Law, 1921, and all subsequent
amendments are hereby repealed.
Read a first time this 28th day of October, 2025 
Read a second time this 28th day of October, 2025 
Read a third time 28th day of October, 2025 
Reconsidered and adopted this 15h day of October, 2025 
MAYOR 
DEPUTY CORPORATE OFFICER 
Signed original bylaw on file
